title: Can you help me practice diagnostic interviewing?

parts.text: Can you help me practice diagnostic interviewing? can you maybe come up with a diagnostic profile for a client but don't reveal any potential diagnosis, and let me try to solve it. we can do the interview as a chat here

I'd love to help you practice diagnostic interviewing! This is a great way to build your skills.

I'll play the role of a client who comes to see you for the first session. I'll respond naturally to your questions based on my client profile, but I won't give you everything upfront - you'll need to ask good questions to gather the information you need.

Let me know when you're ready, and I'll start with what brings me in today. Remember from your course materials:

- Start with open-ended questions, then guide toward specificity
- Explore frequency, duration, intensity, onset
- Cover multiple domains (not just the presenting issue)
- Use closed questions to clarify details
- Assess for suicide and substance use early
- Pay attention to what might rule in or rule out different disorders

Ready? Here we go:

---

**[Session begins]**

*I'm sitting in the waiting room when you come to get me. I look up, make brief eye contact, give a small smile, and follow you back to your office. I sit down and settle in my chair, hands folded in my lap.*

Go ahead - what would you like to ask first?
